title: A Lamp Extinguished
subtitle: The beheading of John the Baptist in Herod's prison, amid a banquet

stillness: Be still, and step away from the banqueting-hall of the world.
Let the music fade; let the lamps grow dim.
Here, in silence, attend to the friend of the Bridegroom.
gospel: When Herod kept the day of his birth,
he made a supper for the princes and chief men of Galilee.
And the daughter of Herodias danced in the midst,
so with an oath he promised to give her whatever she should ask.
Forewarned by her mother, she said:
Give me here on a dish the head of John the Baptist.
And the king was made sorrowful;
yet for his oath, and for those who sat with him at table,
and beheaded John in the prison,
and brought his head on a dish,
and gave it to the girl,
and the girl gave it to her mother.
And his disciples came, and took the body, and buried it,
behold: See how easily sin passes from one into all.
For those who looked on at that spectacle
became sharers in the crime together with those who did it.
Great cruelty, that the holy head was struck off;
greater, that it was carried in and the murder paraded;
greater still, that it was given to a dancing-girl;
but greatest of all, that it was handed into the power of an adulteress.
Behold the bloody banquet borne into the wanton feast,
the unworthy guest upon so worthy a dish.
While Herod feasted, a little before the ninth hour,
holy John the Baptist was beheaded in his cell,
crying out with a loud voice:
My Lord and my God, into your hands I commend my spirit.
And naked under the open sky he lay, despised,
exposed to the gaze of all, until his disciples took him away.
consider: Consider Herod, who was made sorrowful — but feignedly, not truly;
a dissembler of his own mind, he wore grief on his face,
while he carried gladness within.
He excused his crime by an oath,
that under the cloak of piety he might grow the more impious.
In Herod are signified those who, under the appearance of religion,
contrive to do the work of malice;
in Herodias, lust; in the dancing daughter, dissolute desire;
and by these the death of John — that is, of a man full of grace —
is often procured: spiritual death, and sometimes bodily.
He was crafty in promising, and sacrilegious in keeping his word.
O how many subjects today are imperilled in conscience and soul,
because of the unjust deeds of those who rule over them!
In Herod the lesser sin became the cause of the greater:
because he did not quench his lustful will, he came at last to lust;
and because he did not curb his lust, he sank to the guilt of murder.
Wine and women make wise men fall away.
Behold, then, the disciples' devotion to their master:
they came swiftly, and bore him reverently,
and buried him humanely, and announcing it, came in compassion;
and by this office of love they were counted worthy
to pass over into the fellowship of Christ.
prayer: into your hands I commend my spirit.
As John, friend of the Bridegroom, gave his life for the truth,
so let me not be ashamed of the truth,
nor wear gladness within while feigning grief without.
Quench in me the lustful will before it becomes lust,
and curb the lust before it sinks me into deeper guilt.
Grant me to come swiftly to you, and to follow reverently,
passing over, by this office of love, into your fellowship.
My Lord and my God, into your hands I commend my spirit.

latinJewel.latin: Convivante itaque Herode, paulo ante horam diei nonam, sanctus Joannes Baptista est decollatus in custodia, clamando voce magna: Domine Deus meus, tibi commendo spiritum meum.
latinJewel.english: And so, while Herod feasted, a little before the ninth hour, holy John the Baptist was beheaded in his cell, crying out with a loud voice: My Lord and my God, into your hands I commend my spirit.
